speaker: KEEPER
text: Why looks your Grace so heavily today?

speaker: CLARENCE
text: O, I have passed a miserable night,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: So full of fearful dreams, of ugly sights,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: That, as I am a Christian faithful man,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: I would not spend another such a night

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Though ’twere to buy a world of happy days,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: So full of dismal terror was the time.

speaker: KEEPER
text: What was your dream, my lord? I pray you tell me.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Methoughts that I had broken from the Tower

speaker: CLARENCE
text: And was embarked to cross to Burgundy,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: And in my company my brother Gloucester,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Who from my cabin tempted me to walk

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Upon the hatches. Thence we looked toward England

speaker: CLARENCE
text: And cited up a thousand heavy times,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: During the wars of York and Lancaster,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: That had befall’n us. As we paced along

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Upon the giddy footing of the hatches,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Methought that Gloucester stumbled, and in falling

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Struck me, that thought to stay him, overboard

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Into the tumbling billows of the main.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: O Lord, methought what pain it was to drown,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: What dreadful noise of waters in my ears,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: What sights of ugly death within my eyes.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Methoughts I saw a thousand fearful wracks,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: A thousand men that fishes gnawed upon,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Wedges of gold, great anchors, heaps of pearl,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Inestimable stones, unvalued jewels,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: All scattered in the bottom of the sea.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Some lay in dead men’s skulls, and in the holes

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Where eyes did once inhabit, there were crept—

speaker: CLARENCE
text: As ’twere in scorn of eyes—reflecting gems,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: That wooed the slimy bottom of the deep

speaker: CLARENCE
text: And mocked the dead bones that lay scattered by.

speaker: KEEPER
text: Had you such leisure in the time of death

speaker: KEEPER
text: To gaze upon these secrets of the deep?

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Methought I had, and often did I strive

speaker: CLARENCE
text: To yield the ghost, but still the envious flood

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Stopped in my soul and would not let it forth

speaker: CLARENCE
text: To find the empty, vast, and wand’ring air,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: But smothered it within my panting bulk,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Who almost burst to belch it in the sea.

speaker: KEEPER
text: Awaked you not in this sore agony?

speaker: CLARENCE
text: No, no, my dream was lengthened after life.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: O, then began the tempest to my soul.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: I passed, methought, the melancholy flood,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: With that sour ferryman which poets write of,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Unto the kingdom of perpetual night.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: The first that there did greet my stranger-soul

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Was my great father-in-law, renownèd Warwick,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Who spake aloud “What scourge for perjury

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Can this dark monarchy afford false Clarence?”

speaker: CLARENCE
text: And so he vanished. Then came wand’ring by

speaker: CLARENCE
text: A shadow like an angel, with bright hair

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Dabbled in blood, and he shrieked out aloud

speaker: CLARENCE
text: “Clarence is come—false, fleeting, perjured Clarence,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: That stabbed me in the field by Tewkesbury.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Seize on him, furies. Take him unto torment.”

speaker: CLARENCE
text: With that, methoughts, a legion of foul fiends

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Environed me and howlèd in mine ears

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Such hideous cries that with the very noise

speaker: CLARENCE
text: I trembling waked, and for a season after

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Could not believe but that I was in hell,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Such terrible impression made my dream.

speaker: KEEPER
text: No marvel, lord, though it affrighted you.

speaker: KEEPER
text: I am afraid, methinks, to hear you tell it.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Ah keeper, keeper, I have done these things,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: That now give evidence against my soul,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: For Edward’s sake, and see how he requites me.—

speaker: CLARENCE
text: O God, if my deep prayers cannot appease thee,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: But thou wilt be avenged on my misdeeds,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Yet execute thy wrath in me alone!

speaker: CLARENCE
text: O, spare my guiltless wife and my poor children!—

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Keeper, I prithee sit by me awhile.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: My soul is heavy, and I fain would sleep.

speaker: KEEPER
text: I will, my lord. God give your Grace good rest.

speaker: BRAKENBURY
text: Sorrow breaks seasons and reposing hours,

speaker: BRAKENBURY
text: Makes the night morning, and the noontide night.

speaker: BRAKENBURY
text: Princes have but their titles for their glories,

speaker: BRAKENBURY
text: An outward honor for an inward toil,

speaker: BRAKENBURY
text: And, for unfelt imaginations,

speaker: BRAKENBURY
text: They often feel a world of restless cares,

speaker: BRAKENBURY
text: So that between their titles and low name

speaker: BRAKENBURY
text: There’s nothing differs but the outward fame.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: Ho, who’s here?

speaker: BRAKENBURY
text: What wouldst thou, fellow? And how cam’st thou hither?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: I would speak with Clarence, and I

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: came hither on my legs.

speaker: BRAKENBURY
text: What, so brief?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: ’Tis better, sir, than to be tedious.—

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: Let him see our commission, and talk no more.

speaker: BRAKENBURY
text: I am in this commanded to deliver

speaker: BRAKENBURY
text: The noble Duke of Clarence to your hands.

speaker: BRAKENBURY
text: I will not reason what is meant hereby

speaker: BRAKENBURY
text: Because I will be guiltless from the meaning.

speaker: BRAKENBURY
text: There lies the Duke asleep, and there the keys.

speaker: BRAKENBURY
text: I’ll to the King and signify to him

speaker: BRAKENBURY
text: That thus I have resigned to you my charge.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: You may, sir. ’Tis a point of wisdom.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: Fare you well.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: What, shall I stab him as he

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: sleeps?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: No. He’ll say ’twas done cowardly,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: when he wakes.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: Why, he shall never wake until the

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: great Judgment Day.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: Why, then he’ll say we stabbed him

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: sleeping.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: The urging of that word “judgment”

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: hath bred a kind of remorse in me.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: What, art thou afraid?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: Not to kill him, having a warrant,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: but to be damned for killing him, from the which

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: no warrant can defend me.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: I thought thou hadst been resolute.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: So I am—to let him live.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: I’ll back to the Duke of Gloucester

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: and tell him so.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: Nay, I prithee stay a little. I hope

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: this passionate humor of mine will change. It was

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: wont to hold me but while one tells twenty.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: How dost thou feel thyself now?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: Faith, some certain dregs of conscience

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: are yet within me.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: Remember our reward when the

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: deed’s done.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: Zounds, he dies! I had forgot the

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: reward.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: Where’s thy conscience now?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: O, in the Duke of Gloucester’s

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: purse.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: When he opens his purse to give us

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: our reward, thy conscience flies out.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: ’Tis no matter. Let it go. There’s

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: few or none will entertain it.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: What if it come to thee again?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: I’ll not meddle with it. It makes a

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: man a coward: a man cannot steal but it accuseth

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: him; a man cannot swear but it checks him; a man

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: cannot lie with his neighbor’s wife but it detects

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: him. ’Tis a blushing, shamefaced spirit that mutinies

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: in a man’s bosom. It fills a man full of

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: obstacles. It made me once restore a purse of gold

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: that by chance I found. It beggars any man that

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: keeps it. It is turned out of towns and cities for a

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: dangerous thing, and every man that means to live

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: well endeavors to trust to himself and live without it.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: Zounds, ’tis even now at my elbow,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: persuading me not to kill the Duke.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: Take the devil in thy mind, and

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: believe him not. He would insinuate with thee but

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: to make thee sigh.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: I am strong-framed. He cannot prevail

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: with me.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: Spoke like a tall man that respects

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: thy reputation. Come, shall we fall to work?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: Take him on the costard with the

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: hilts of thy sword, and then throw him into the

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: malmsey butt in the next room.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: O, excellent device—and make a

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: sop of him!

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: Soft, he wakes.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: Strike!

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: No, we’ll reason with him.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Where art thou, keeper? Give me a cup of wine.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: You shall have wine enough, my lord, anon.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: In God’s name, what art thou?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: A man, as you are.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: But not, as I am, royal.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: Nor you, as we are, loyal.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Thy voice is thunder, but thy looks are humble.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: My voice is now the King’s, my looks mine own.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: How darkly and how deadly dost thou speak!

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Your eyes do menace me. Why look you pale?

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Who sent you hither? Wherefore do you come?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: To, to, to—

speaker: CLARENCE
text: To murder me?

speaker: BOTH
text: Ay, ay.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: You scarcely have the hearts to tell me so

speaker: CLARENCE
text: And therefore cannot have the hearts to do it.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Wherein, my friends, have I offended you?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: Offended us you have not, but the King.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: I shall be reconciled to him again.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: Never, my lord. Therefore prepare to die.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Are you drawn forth among a world of men

speaker: CLARENCE
text: To slay the innocent? What is my offense?

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Where is the evidence that doth accuse me?

speaker: CLARENCE
text: What lawful quest have given their verdict up

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Unto the frowning judge? Or who pronounced

speaker: CLARENCE
text: The bitter sentence of poor Clarence’ death

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Before I be convict by course of law?

speaker: CLARENCE
text: To threaten me with death is most unlawful.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: I charge you, as you hope to have redemption,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: By Christ’s dear blood shed for our grievous sins,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: That you depart, and lay no hands on me.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: The deed you undertake is damnable.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: What we will do, we do upon command.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: And he that hath commanded is our king.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Erroneous vassals, the great King of kings

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Hath in the table of His law commanded

speaker: CLARENCE
text: That thou shalt do no murder. Will you then

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Spurn at His edict and fulfill a man’s?

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Take heed, for He holds vengeance in His hand

speaker: CLARENCE
text: To hurl upon their heads that break His law.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: And that same vengeance doth He hurl on thee

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: For false forswearing and for murder too.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: Thou didst receive the sacrament to fight

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: In quarrel of the House of Lancaster.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: And, like a traitor to the name of God,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: Didst break that vow, and with thy treacherous blade

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: Unrippedst the bowels of thy sovereign’s son.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: Whom thou wast sworn to cherish and defend.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: How canst thou urge God’s dreadful law to us

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: When thou hast broke it in such dear degree?

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Alas! For whose sake did I that ill deed?

speaker: CLARENCE
text: For Edward, for my brother, for his sake.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: He sends you not to murder me for this,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: For in that sin he is as deep as I.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: If God will be avengèd for the deed,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: O, know you yet He doth it publicly!

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Take not the quarrel from His powerful arm;

speaker: CLARENCE
text: He needs no indirect or lawless course

speaker: CLARENCE
text: To cut off those that have offended Him.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: Who made thee then a bloody minister

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: When gallant-springing, brave Plantagenet,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: That princely novice, was struck dead by thee?

speaker: CLARENCE
text: My brother’s love, the devil, and my rage.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: Thy brother’s love, our duty, and thy faults

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: Provoke us hither now to slaughter thee.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: If you do love my brother, hate not me.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: I am his brother, and I love him well.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: If you are hired for meed, go back again,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: And I will send you to my brother Gloucester,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Who shall reward you better for my life

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Than Edward will for tidings of my death.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: You are deceived. Your brother Gloucester hates you.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: O no, he loves me, and he holds me dear.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Go you to him from me.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: Ay, so we will.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Tell him, when that our princely father York

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Blessed his three sons with his victorious arm,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: He little thought of this divided friendship.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Bid Gloucester think of this, and he will weep.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: Ay, millstones, as he lessoned us to weep.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: O, do not slander him, for he is kind.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: Right, as snow in harvest. Come, you deceive yourself.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: ’Tis he that sends us to destroy you here.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: It cannot be, for he bewept my fortune,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: And hugged me in his arms, and swore with sobs

speaker: CLARENCE
text: That he would labor my delivery.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: Why, so he doth, when he delivers you

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: From this Earth’s thralldom to the joys of heaven.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: Make peace with God, for you must die, my lord.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Have you that holy feeling in your souls

speaker: CLARENCE
text: To counsel me to make my peace with God,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: And are you yet to your own souls so blind

speaker: CLARENCE
text: That you will war with God by murd’ring me?

speaker: CLARENCE
text: O sirs, consider: they that set you on

speaker: CLARENCE
text: To do this deed will hate you for the deed.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: What shall we do?

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Relent, and save your souls.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Which of you—if you were a prince’s son

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Being pent from liberty, as I am now—

speaker: CLARENCE
text: If two such murderers as yourselves came to you,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Would not entreat for life? Ay, you would beg,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Were you in my distress.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: Relent? No. ’Tis cowardly and womanish.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Not to relent is beastly, savage, devilish.

speaker: CLARENCE
text:
To
                 
                Second
                 
                Murderer
                .
                 
              My friend, I spy some pity in thy looks.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: O, if thine eye be not a flatterer,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Come thou on my side and entreat for me.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: A begging prince what beggar pities not?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: Look behind you, my lord.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text:
Take that, and that.
                 
                (
                Stabs
                 
                him
                .
                )
               If all this will not do,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: I’ll drown you in the malmsey butt within.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: A bloody deed, and desperately dispatched.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: How fain, like Pilate, would I wash my hands

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: Of this most grievous murder.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: How now? What mean’st thou that thou help’st me not?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: By heavens, the Duke shall know how slack you have been.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: I would he knew that I had saved his brother.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: Take thou the fee, and tell him what I say,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: For I repent me that the Duke is slain.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: So do not I. Go, coward as thou art.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: Well, I’ll go hide the body in some hole

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: Till that the Duke give order for his burial.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: And when I have my meed, I will away,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: For this will out, and then I must not stay.
